concepts.name: Object
concepts.definition: Reflective base instance that exposes named property access and optional asset identity.
concepts.boundary: Reflective base instance, named property mutation, and optional asset identity.

concepts.name: TypeInfo
concepts.definition: Static reflection metadata describing a class, its parent, declared properties, and factory.
concepts.boundary: Static class metadata, parent lineage, property descriptors, and factory pointer.

concepts.name: TypeRegistry
concepts.definition: Global registry that stores reflected classes and creates objects by type name.
concepts.boundary: Global registry for reflected classes, constructor lookup, and type enumeration.

concepts.name: AssetUID
concepts.definition: Stable numeric identifier for an asset across persistence and lookup flows.
concepts.boundary: Stable 64-bit identifier for persisted asset references.

concepts.name: AssetHeader
concepts.definition: Fixed metadata header stored ahead of an .ome asset body.
concepts.boundary: Fixed ome header with asset kind, uid, dependency list, and blueprint type tag.

concepts.name: AssetWriteTransaction
concepts.definition: Transactional file writer that stages a new asset body before commit or rollback.
concepts.boundary: Staged file writer using temp paths, backups, and atomic commit rollback.

concepts.name: Serializer
concepts.definition: Binary persistence service for reflected objects and asset-linked references.
concepts.boundary: Binary persistence for payload values, schema tags, and uid-backed links.

concepts.name: Component
concepts.definition: Entity-attached reflective unit that can be toggled on or off.
concepts.boundary: Entity-attached unit with enabled gate for ecs composition.

concepts.name: Transform
concepts.definition: Spatial component carrying position, rotation, and scale values.
concepts.boundary: Spatial coordinates, rotation, and scale payload for entities.

concepts.name: EngineContext
concepts.definition: Runtime composition root that exposes the active engine services and extensions.
concepts.boundary: Startup-owned service hub exposing window, input, renderer, viewport, world, and localization handles.

concepts.name: FrameLoop
concepts.definition: Main runtime loop that sequences tick phases for windowed or headless execution.
concepts.boundary: Window and headless tick orchestration, callback phases, and delta-step sequencing.

concepts.name: IRenderer
concepts.definition: Abstract rendering contract for initialization, frame lifecycle, scene submission, and export.
concepts.boundary: Abstract rendering contract covering frame lifecycle and scene submission.

concepts.name: SceneRenderHook
concepts.definition: Callback extension point invoked during scene rendering with raw GPU context.
concepts.boundary: Callback injection point with raw gpu context during scene draws.

concepts.name: AnimationGraph
concepts.definition: Authored animation state graph describing clip transitions, parameters, and events.
concepts.boundary: Animation state machine topology, transition rules, clip wiring, and event markers.

concepts.name: EffectSystem
concepts.definition: Runtime service that routes entity-authored and explicit effect playback through a backend.
concepts.boundary: Effect playback routing, backend dispatch, authored effect components, and lifetime updates.

concepts.name: Ref
concepts.definition: Non-owning object or asset reference wrapper that can carry an AssetUID.
concepts.boundary: Stored wrapper for object fields, serialized pointers, and deferred uid linkage.

concepts.name: AssetHandle
concepts.definition: Runtime handle that resolves an asset identity through registry-managed lookup.
concepts.boundary: Runtime resolution handle for path lookup, cache access, and deferred materialization.

concepts.name: World
concepts.definition: Runtime ECS container managing entities, component storage, and world transactions.
concepts.boundary: Runtime world containing entities, components, and mutation transactions.

concepts.name: WorldResource
concepts.definition: Serialized world root asset that identifies the base level input and optional entity manifest overlays used to build a world instance.
concepts.boundary: Serialized world root naming the level reference and overlay file path.

concepts.name: Level
concepts.definition: Serialized gameplay template carrying gravity and entity budget defaults.
concepts.boundary: Gameplay template storing gravity, entity budget, and initialization defaults.

concepts.name: EntityManifest
concepts.definition: Declarative world composition document that appends authored entities, reflected components, and property values into a runtime world.
concepts.boundary: Overlay schema, class identifiers, and field assignments for appended authored bundles.

concepts.name: LoadedModule
concepts.definition: Native module lifetime wrapper that owns a loaded binary handle and exported factories.
concepts.boundary: Native module lifetime, binary library ownership, exported factory discovery, and unload safety.

concepts.name: IGame
concepts.definition: Versioned gameplay interface implemented by a game module and driven by the engine.
concepts.boundary: Versioned gameplay entry callbacks for start, tick, destroy, and debug-ui negotiation.

concepts.name: EngineExtensionSlot
concepts.definition: Per-engine attachment slot that lets external modules hang opaque state off EngineContext.
concepts.boundary: Type-keyed or named attachment slots for module-owned state and custom deleters.

concepts.name: EncounterZone
concepts.definition: World-authored grouping that scopes encounter generators, anchors, objectives, and activation tags.
concepts.boundary: Encounter-scoped cluster of lanes, origins, objectives, and zone labels for authored spaces.

concepts.name: SpawnSource
concepts.definition: Placed world entity that describes a reusable generation source for any runtime payload.
concepts.boundary: Placed emission descriptor: tag bindings, catalog linkage, trigger ownership, and emit lanes.

concepts.name: SpawnAnchor
concepts.definition: Semantic origin marker used by a generation source to place results in meaningful space.
concepts.boundary: Semantic origin marker such as front flank rear objective or reinforcement lane.

concepts.name: SpawnVolume
concepts.definition: Authored area sampler that yields valid placement positions for a generation source.
concepts.boundary: Area sampler: shape rules, distribution map, exclusion padding, and occupancy tests.

concepts.name: SpawnPayloadSet
concepts.definition: Reusable catalog of spawnable payload entries, weights, costs, and role tags.
concepts.boundary: Entry table of weighted prefabs, costs, quotas, and role labels.

concepts.name: SpawnCondition
concepts.definition: Declarative trigger rule describing when a generation source may emit outputs.
concepts.boundary: Declarative trigger predicates based on stage state, objective state, time windows, and tag gates.

concepts.name: SpawnLimit
concepts.definition: Authored limit policy constraining cooldown, concurrency, count, and budget of a generation source.
concepts.boundary: Quota policy: recharge gate, concurrency ceiling, lifetime allotment, and budget ceiling.

concepts.name: SpawnDirector
concepts.definition: Runtime policy that evaluates pacing and requests eligible world-authored generation sources.
concepts.boundary: Pacing policy that ranks eligible emitters from danger, tempo, and objective pressure.

concepts.name: SpawnRequest
concepts.definition: Single runtime request instructing the spawn framework to activate a source with specific intent.
concepts.boundary: Single dispatch order naming a source, desired count, and placement pattern.

concepts.name: SpawnRuntimeState
concepts.definition: Per-source runtime state tracking cooldowns, remaining charges, active outputs, and activation flags.
concepts.boundary: Live emission ledger: recharge clock, remaining charges, live occupancy, and enable flags.

concepts.name: SpawnedActorLink
concepts.definition: Runtime association from a spawned actor back to the source instance that emitted it.
concepts.boundary: Association from a live actor back to its emitter lineage token.
